This '''Great Hyperspace War Bas-Relief''' was a [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] frieze, excavated from an archaeological dig on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].

==History==
[[File:GreatHyperspaceWarBasRelief-BotE.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A bas-relief in the Imperial Citadel on Dromund Kaas.]]
In [[3678 BBY]], this art piece, or a similar rendering of it, was displayed along the wall of the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor's]] chambers in the [[Imperial Citadel (Sith Empire)|Dromund Kaas citadel]] in [[Kaas City]].<ref name="The Old Republic, Blood of the Empire Act 1" />

At some point by [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], it was found in an archaeological excavation of a [[Massassi Site/Legends|Massassi site]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] then put it on display in [[Chancellor's Suite/Legends|his suite]].<ref name="CL" />

It depicted a battle which took place during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], with [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] clashing,<ref name="CL" /> alongside various war beasts.<ref name="ROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref> Jedi visitors to his office believed that it was a nod of respect towards their order, but it was actually a statement of Palpatine's belief in Sith superiority.<ref name="BTS" />

By the aftermath of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[0 BBY/Legends|nineteen years]] after Palpatine [[Declaration of a New Order|declared himself]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]], Palpatine's ownership of the bas-relief had spread via rumors.<ref name="SWG">{{SWGTCG|set=Champions of the Force (SWGTCG)|cardname=Great Hyperspace War Bas-Relief|swglink=Great hyperspace war bas relief.png}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Palp-frieze_concept.jpg|thumb|left|260px|The original concept art made for the frieze during production of ''Revenge of the Sith'']]
{{Quote|The idea behind the archeological frieze in Palpatine's office is that it depicts an event. George was very clear about making it a dynamic and somewhat gory scene of Jedi and aliens and warriors fighting each other.|Erik Tiemens|<ref name="Art">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith]]''</ref>}}
The day before the shooting of the scene in Palpatine's private office [[George Lucas]] decided to put an elaborated sculpture in the hallway. The frieze was drawn by design supervisor [[Erik Tiemens]] and sculpted by Richard Miller, depicting a legendary or historical battle between aliens and humans, with some of the aliens resembling "grays",<ref name="Creating the Worlds of Star Wars: 365 Days">''[[Creating the Worlds of Star Wars: 365 Days]]''</ref> the almond-eyed creatures of classic UFO lore. While during the time of development there wasn't much explanation of the frieze's meaning,<ref name="Insider92">[[Star Wars Insider 92|''Star Wars Insider'' 92]]</ref> later Expanded Universe material identified it as a portrayal of the Great Hyperspace War between Jedi and Sith.<ref name="SWG" />

Included in both versions of the piece is a female warrior curiously similar to concept art depicting a red-haired "[[Nightsister Sith witch|Sith Witch]]," which was rejected as a villain in the [[prequel trilogy]].<ref name="art">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace]]''</ref> Whether this was an intentional nod to the character is unknown.
